entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A major element and nutrient that limits the production of cereals.

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Agricultural crops whose production is limited by nitrogen availability.

entities.name: NITROGEN CYCLE
entities.description: The process of nitrogen turnover that affects the availability of nitrogen in the soil.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form by organisms, also known as BNF.

entities.name: FERTILISER APPLICATION
entities.description: The process of adding nitrogen to the soil via fertilizers.

entities.name: GRASSES
entities.description: Forage plants associated with nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: NON-LEGUMES
entities.description: Plants that are not legumes but can still exhibit nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: NITROGEN BALANCE STUDIES
entities.description: Studies used to obtain reliable estimates of nitrogen fixation in the field.

entities.name: PLANT
entities.description: The host organism that forms an association with nitrogen-fixing bacteria.

entities.name: NITROGEN-FIXING BACTERIA
entities.description: Bacteria that associate with plants to fix nitrogen.

entities.name: ISOLATION
entities.description: The process of separating nitrogen-fixing bacteria from their environment.

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating nitrogen-fixing bacteria for specific traits.
